ranked_movies.title: The Gangster, the Cop, the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: The original post that sparked the main discussion. It is explicitly praised as pure entertainment with crazy action and is the central point of reference for the entire thread.

ranked_movies.title: I Saw the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a benchmark for high-quality, intense Korean thrillers. It's cited as an influence on other films and is a highly recommended classic.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: A cornerstone of modern Korean cinema. It's directly named and its iconic status is highlighted by its inclusion in a list of films that inspired Indian remakes.

ranked_movies.title: The Man from Nowhere
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently recommended as a top-tier action film. Its influence is noted by its Indian remake, 'Rocky Handsome', solidifying its must-watch status.

ranked_movies.title: A Bittersweet Life
ranked_movies.reason: Explicitly stated as an 'obvious' recommendation in the action genre. It's praised for its style and is another film noted for inspiring an Indian version.

ranked_movies.title: Memories of Murder
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a high-water mark for Korean filmmaking, used as a comparison point to show the caliber of cinema the country produces.

ranked_movies.title: The Outlaws
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a perfect entertaining action popcorn movie, serving as the first film in the popular 'The Roundup' series.

ranked_movies.title: The Chaser
ranked_movies.reason: Its status as a classic is confirmed by its inclusion on the list of influential Korean films that were remade in India ('Murder 2').

ranked_movies.title: Save the Green Planet!
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed at length as the original South Korean film that the high-profile Hollywood remake 'Bugonia' (by Yorgos Lanthimos) is based on, generating significant buzz.

ranked_movies.title: City of Violence
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily praised in the comments, with one user calling it the best fight scenes in Korean film history and their personal introduction to Korean content.

ranked_movies.title: The Good, the Bad, the Weird
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended and loved for its unique and entertaining take on the western genre, described as a great movie.

ranked_movies.title: A Dirty Carnival
ranked_movies.reason: Included on the list of seminal Korean films that inspired other international productions, specifically the Tamil film 'Jigarthanda'.

ranked_movies.title: My Sassy Girl
ranked_movies.reason: A iconic romantic comedy whose influence is noted by its Indian remake, 'Ugly aur Pagli', demonstrating its cross-cultural appeal.

ranked_movies.title: Nameless Gangster
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended with enthusiasm ('ftw') in the main discussion thread, indicating it's a well-regarded entry in the gangster genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Ring Virus
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of multiple posts asking for opinions on it. It is the South Korean remake of 'Ringu' and sparked detailed comparisons between the Japanese, American, and Korean versions.

ranked_movies.title: A Hard Day
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically named in the main list of recommendations, signifying it's a solid choice for fans of the action-thriller genre.

ranked_movies.title: Shiri
ranked_movies.reason: Acknowledged for its historical impact on the Korean film industry, described as having an 'immeasurable' impact despite not being a perfect movie.

ranked_movies.title: Nowhere to Hide
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as an often-forgotten gem from the Korean film boom that is still highly entertaining and aesthetically influential (inspiring 'The Matrix').

ranked_movies.title: Extreme Job
ranked_movies.reason: Listed among a group of films recommended for pure entertainment value, known for its comedy and action.

ranked_movies.title: Mercy for None
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as a TV series with a feature film level of action, described as non-stop action and gore, akin to a 'South Korean John Wick'.
